metadata.title: Part 1: Chapter-by-Chapter Summaries

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The frequentist interpretation promised empiricism by defining probability as long-run frequency. The chapter traces four major interpretations, each persuasive until it is pressed on a hard case. Chapter 2 springs a trap, exposing where the confident frequentist reading breaks down. Adolf Quetelet bridged urn problems to social science with l'homme moyen, the average man as a statistical center of gravity.

artifactLines: The frequentist interpretation promised empiricism by defining probability as long-run frequency.
The chapter traces four major interpretations, each persuasive until it is pressed on a hard case.
Chapter 2 springs a trap, exposing where the confident frequentist reading breaks down.
Adolf Quetelet bridged urn problems to social science with l'homme moyen, the average man as a statistical center of gravity.

beats.narration_text: The frequentist promise that probability equals long-run frequency, then Chapter 2 springing the trap, and Quetelet building l'homme moyen as a statistical center of gravity, makes me want to trace where each interpretation breaks. Can you (1) state the four interpretations of probability the chapter names and the single case that pressures each, (2) show concretely how the bridge from urn problems to social science let Quetelet's average man emerge, and (3) walk me through the trap Chapter 2 springs using a worked frequentist example that fails?

beats.narration_text: The journey from urn problems to social science required a bridge, and Belgian scientist Adolf Quetelet built it. His conceptual innovation was “l’homme moyen,” the average man, a statistical center of gravity around which individuals varied.

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — I'm building a [game / tool / system]. Extract the three most surprising design decisions from Part 1: Chapter-by-Chapter… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: I'm building a [game / tool / system]. Extract the three most surprising design decisions from Part 1: Chapter-by-Chapter Summaries and tell me one thing I should do differently because of each.
